name: I hurt my neck at work but it didn't hurt until the next day—what should I do?

mainEntity.name: I hurt my neck at work but it didn't hurt until the next day—what should I do?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report the injury to your boss immediately and see a doctor. It's common for neck pain and whiplash symptoms to be delayed, but you need a medical record to link the pain to your shift.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Neck injuries often involve soft tissue (like whiplash) where symptoms—such as headaches, numbness, or stiffness—don't peak for 24-48 hours. You should still report the incident to your supervisor immediately, even if you just 'feel a little stiff.' Seeking medical attention at an urgent care or ER is vital to create a timeline. In Utah and Idaho, the 'nexus' or link between the work event and the neck pain is more easily proven if you seek help within the first 48 hours.

Key factors include the mechanism of injury (was it a jolt or a fall?) and the speed of your medical follow-up.

Describe the accident clearly: did your head hit something, or was it a sudden 'whiplash' motion? This helps doctors diagnose potential disc issues early.

Because neck injuries can lead to chronic nerve issues, having an attorney ensure the record is clear from day one is a huge advantage.
